package handler

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Positive scenario for auth_or_tenant_extraction_duplicated_across_handlers.
// Rule intent: Repeated auth, tenant, or principal extraction that should live in middleware or a shared helper.
// Anti-pattern: Same tenant/user extraction logic copy-pasted across multiple handlers.

type Document struct {
	ID       uint   `gorm:"primaryKey"`
	Title    string `gorm:"column:title"`
	TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id"`
}

type Report struct {
	ID       uint   `gorm:"primaryKey"`
	Name     string `gorm:"column:name"`
	TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id"`
}

// Duplicated tenant extraction in handler 1
func GetDocumentsHandler(c *gin.Context, db *gorm.DB) {
	tenantID := c.GetHeader("X-Tenant-ID")
	userID := c.GetHeader("X-User-ID")
	if tenantID == "" {
		c.JSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, gin.H{"error": "missing tenant"})
		return
	}

	var docs []Document
	db.Where("tenant_id = ?", tenantID).Find(&docs)
	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, docs)
}

// Duplicated tenant extraction in handler 2
func GetReportsHandler(c *gin.Context, db *gorm.DB) {
	tenantID := c.GetHeader("X-Tenant-ID")
	userID := c.GetHeader("X-User-ID")
	if tenantID == "" {
		c.JSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, gin.H{"error": "missing tenant"})
		return
	}

	var reports []Report
	db.Where("tenant_id = ?", tenantID).Find(&reports)
	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, reports)
}

// Duplicated tenant extraction in handler 3
func CreateDocumentHandler(c *gin.Context, db *gorm.DB) {
	tenantID := c.GetHeader("X-Tenant-ID")
	userID := c.GetHeader("X-User-ID")
	if tenantID == "" {
		c.JSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, gin.H{"error": "missing tenant"})
		return
	}

	var doc Document
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&doc); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}
	doc.TenantID = tenantID
	db.Create(&doc)
	c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, doc)
}
